title: aspidium thwaitesii
intro: Aspidium thwaitesii, also known as Thwaites' fern, is a species of fern native to Sri Lanka. It is traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into a decoction or infused in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed for its medicinal properties.
